description: Compact hamburger menu panel for mobile devices + Floating Action Button (FAB) with settings and AI chatbot. The hamburger menu becomes smaller and simpler. A new floating icon button gives quick access to settings (theme, language, performance) and an AI chatbot powered by OpenAI API with configurable API key.
steps: 1. Simplify src/components/Navbar.tsx mobile menu panel: Reduce the panel width to max-w-[300px]. Make the navigation links more compact (reduce padding, use smaller font). Remove the settings footer from the mobile menu (theme/language) since those will move to the FAB. Keep only navigation links + close button. The panel should open/close faster with simpler animation.

2. Create src/components/FloatingActionButton.tsx - A floating circular button fixed at bottom-right (bottom-6 right-6) with z-index 40. When clicked, it expands into a radial/popup menu showing: (a) Theme toggle (sun/moon icon), (b) Language switcher (globe icon), (c) Performance mode (zap icon), (d) AI Chat (message-circle icon). Use CSS transitions for the expand animation. Each sub-button should be a smaller circle that fans out above the main button.

3. Create src/components/AIChatbot.tsx - A chat panel that slides up from the FAB position. Features: (a) Chat message list with user/bot messages, (b) Input field with send button, (c) Close button to minimize, (d) Messages stored in component state, (e) Integration with OpenAI API (fetch to https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions), (f) API key stored in localStorage (configurable via a settings gear icon in the chat header), (g) System prompt configurable (default: 'You are a helpful assistant for this portfolio website. Answer questions about the developer, their projects, skills, and availability.'), (h) Error handling for missing API key (show a setup prompt), (i) Loading state with typing indicator. Keep the component lightweight - no external chat library.

4. Create src/lib/chatStorage.ts - Utility functions for storing/retrieving OpenAI API key and system prompt from localStorage. Functions: getApiKey(), setApiKey(key), getSystemPrompt(), setSystemPrompt(prompt), getChatHistory(), saveChatHistory(messages).
5. In src/App.tsx, import and render the FloatingActionButton component. Place it after the Navbar. It should only render after the preloader completes (inside the !isLoading block).

6. Add new i18n keys to src/locales/en.json for: fab.theme, fab.language, fab.performance, fab.chat, chat.title, chat.placeholder, chat.send, chat.apiKeyMissing, chat.apiKeySetup, chat.apiKeyLabel, chat.systemPromptLabel, chat.save, chat.typing, chat.error, chat.welcome. Add same keys to id.json and zh.json.
7. In the AIChatbot settings, add a small form where users can paste their OpenAI API key and optionally customize the system prompt. This should be a collapsible section in the chat header.

8. Ensure the FAB and chatbot are performant: use CSS transitions instead of framer-motion, lazy-load the chatbot component only when the chat button is clicked, and keep the FAB itself lightweight (just a button + CSS).
acceptance_criteria: Mobile hamburger menu panel is max 300px wide with only nav links (no settings)
Floating Action Button appears at bottom-right on all devices
FAB expands to show 4 quick-action buttons (theme, language, performance, chat)
Clicking chat opens an AI chatbot panel with message history
Chatbot connects to OpenAI API using user-provided API key from localStorage
API key can be configured via settings in the chat header
System prompt is customizable and defaults to portfolio-focused prompt
Chat shows typing indicator while waiting for API response
Missing API key shows a helpful setup message
